title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan was inducted into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame after a four-night concert run at Fenway Park.
Kahan is the fourth inductee, joining artists like Billy Joel and Paul McCartney in the Hall of Fame.
Governor Maura Healey declared a 'Noah Kahan Day' in recognition of his achievements and concerts.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from Cash App's $45 million multistate settlement over alleged deceptive practices.
The settlement alleges Block Inc. misled consumers about Cash App's safety and failed to protect users from fraud.
Block must improve fraud protection, discontinue risky promotions, and provide 24-hour customer support as part of the settlement.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old Tyngsborough man allegedly operated a police boat on Lake Mascuppic, attempting to enforce local boating rules.
Tyngsborough police plan to charge the man with impersonating an officer, pending a clerk-magistrate's review for sufficient evidence.
Officials have removed the police boat from the dock to prevent further unauthorized use after the incident.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 tall ships and military vessels from 20 countries arrived in Boston Harbor for Sail Boston 250.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail, marking the final stop of the Sail250 tour.
Thousands of visitors gathered across waterfront locations to watch the international ships dock and parade.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox draft shortstop Jake Schaffner from University of North Carolina with No. 20 overall pick in 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th by MLB.com, led off for UNC with a .356 average, 6 HRs, and 50 RBIs in 68 games.
Schaffner shows strong defensive skills with a .987 fielding percentage and potential switch to second base in pro ball.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston 2026 event showcased the Grand Parade of Sail, led by the USS Constitution.
Historic ships from around the world, including the replica schooner Bluenose Two, participated in the parade.
The event marked the 250th anniversary, with ties to maritime history and future sailing generations.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello awarded midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' by The Athletic for posting a 10.35 ERA in eight starts.
Bello allowed 41 earned runs over 35.2 innings, with batters hitting .365 against him before demotion.
Despite poor starts, Bello excelled as a reliever, posting a 0.71 ERA in 25.1 relief innings for the Red Sox.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox face a dilemma at 2026 trade deadline, unsure whether to buy into a postseason run or sell assets.
Boston is on a seven-game winning streak, placing them less than two games back from the American League's final wild-card spot.
Craig Breslow's future and Red Sox's strategy will depend on whether recent performance is a turnaround or a temporary streak.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden in Boston, part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour.
The concert setlist combined new tracks from her latest album with classics like 'Hips Don't Lie'.
Shakira highlighted resilience and empowerment themes, supported by elaborate stage effects and diverse dance styles.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Healey announces $18.6 million in tax credits for 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester involve converting historic buildings into apartments.
The Housing Development Incentive Program supports housing for cities with high housing costs and home shortages.
